column23: Comments

Loved this game! The card draw you got from Jubilee really made the Banshee/Phoenix/Psylocke deck work. Add the hyperspeed/dark memories engine from the Darkhawk/Ronin deck to the mix and the villains stood no change. Could have ended it sooner if I didn't get to greedy. Could have had the final blow four turns earlier, but decided to kill some villains. Next two turns were master strikes... Hopefully this doesn't bite me in the standings.

An interesting setup. Piercing made it a bit easy in a lot of ways, but definitely seemed the way to go for hero choices. Funnest part was when Ollie played the Phoenix rare to deal the final blow and was literally 1 attack point away from being corrupted by power.

Piercing energy was really effective at saving Jubilee cards from villains, and we had to show a lot of restraint in leaving henchmen and low VP cards in the city to ensure Jubilee wasn’t captured by Mysterious instead. Very lucky villain deck in that twists came out mid-late game.

First play of this set up was nothing more then a flaming bag of garbage I forgot that Phoenix K.O.s alot and got her ult card. You can uhh piece together the rest. 

Now the main game this game went so well. All the bystanders rescued by Jean along with phylocks piercing energy made for one hell of a deck. Then the war machine ronan combo was perfect with dark memories coming back to bite the hood gang. And in the end I had all 5 master strikes in my victory pile.

The setup was nice although these self-KO Phoenix cards had the potential to end the game if not too careful. Left side focused on PE, Right - mainly on Lightshow. Ronin was the outlier but helped in one or two ocasions. I was lucky to pull only 1 MS before end of game which made this quick win possible.
Banshee is the goat. Hellcat unfortunately did not pull her weight which was pretty disappointing. I never got enough yellow to make it work. Probably could have went with Polaris instead.

column2: Nick Fury Ruins the Perfect Wedding
column23: Really fun setup! I've always loved this scheme because it requires constant attention, but it's beatable as long as you manage your decisions. Funnily enough, both runs ended up with nearly identical results.

I was nervous going into this as I was facing a mastermind I had never faced with a scheme I had never played using 3 heroes I had never used before. Things seemed to go well with one hand getting Electros Supercharge on their 3rd turn and then focusing on dodge while the other hand used doc oc to keep the city under control and poke Nick Fury a few times.

I tried to make new recruits happen and they did a little pit but the big hitter was Trample underhoof getting 6 from Lok's rare, that and goblin gliders dodging a lot of base cards gave player 2 a deck that consistently hit over 9. Nick Fury had some well time command strikes to extend the  game though. I had an empty city most of the game so that hurt my score for point per turn.

Love this scheme. Lots of early scheme twists helped me get the wedding heroes.  I kept the city spaces clear whenever a scheme twist happen so only 2 wedding heroes were KOed the whole game. First turn masterstrike was nice. Due to all the early twists and masterstrikes not as many adversaries entered the city and that hurt my vp total.

Tried to put all of the Strength cards that cost 4 or more on one side, to maximize we Juggernauts impact and be able to trigger strength requirements easily. Majority of what was left for the other side was Green Goblin and recruit cards, which allowed them to run a powerful dodge strategy to get to rares quickly!!

column2: Galactus Transforms Commuters into Giant Ants
column23: Ant-Man and Forge, with a little help from Domino, shoved those giant ants down Galactus’ throat, and surprisingly, it was too much for him.
Played my whole deck through twice this game, had three 7 cost cards (Black Panther, Domino and Apocalyptic Kitty Pryde)... but my wife still way outscored me! it was a lot of fun!!

Apparently Galactus had never heard of science. All the Tech card from Domino, S. Iron Man and Ant-Man made Galactus a surprising pushover. The other side scrapped together a Ranged and Instinct deck, with Blade mostly watching from the sidelines. THREE Amy's pushed out two Villains on the second to last turn as a part of seven bystanders being rescued from "Panicked Mobs."

Galactus came out the gate destroying three city spaces which caused a lot of escapes, but Doctor Strange stole the whole show and gave Galactus a bargain he couldn't refused. And the bargain looked like a big magical fist.

omg, everything was looking very grim! my first hq looked great but it almost instantly got put to the bottom by one of the heralds, and in the last turn electro revealed the final master strike to blow up the whole city, but smh i combo'd on this exact turn, lowering galactuss's power to 5 and gaining a whopping 28 attack total! not sure if my hero choices where the best but they did the job (first try), 2nd game i lost turn 23 due to the scheme

3 Twists in a row when there were no Ants stacked next to the Mastermind, along with two of the weaker hands I've had for the whole game snatched defeat from the jaws of victory, with only the Final Blow left. I really hope my PPT is high enough to see me through, otherwise I'm bowing out of this tournament earlier than last time.
Galactus beat my rear end the second play through. Luckily, I had a win the first play through.

I had fun. The mixture of bribe villains with traditionally green high attack masterminds and villain themed set up was nice, didn't punish us so much for having recruit in our decks. Played well with She-Hulk.

Picked Nightcrawler to be able to more easily negate the Scheme effects, by means of Teleport. Then added more covert (with Caiera) to have a better chance of activating Nightcrawlers uncommon. Turned out that Nightcrawler and Sentry actually did most of the heavy lifting!!
Wanted to try some topdeck stuff, both black cat and sentry leads to it

Very nice setup! I discovered that Sentry is best, when you use Angel's Drop off a friend and discard his high cost transformed cards ^^ Gladiator Hulk was a good partner for She-Hulk. Didn't need Black Cat at all, she is one of the weakest heroes! 2nd match had more VP but one escape - I hope I submitted the best result ;-)
Smash smash smash

More or less the exact same cards came out in the HQ from my first attempt. I just manage to keep getting the KO strikes instead of the wounding strikes and I studied more so Outwit helped thin my deck enough for the lone Diving Catch to trigger nearly every twist past the first 2.
I hate this scheme.
Team Elektrospidey hit mm on turn 5. Team Iceman ended up hitting mm only once because Bishop decided he is not gonna show up.
Not many victory points in the villain deck. The villain combination was not that challenging

Stryfe didn't stand a chance. Reality Gem tucked master strikes, Cable's 'Disaster Recovery' triggered, tactics whiffed. 4 early twists gave me plenty of shards to spend. Forge & Punisher make a great team, so do Bishop & Cable. 'Thwip!' came out early and was the only Spidey card I would've wanted. Very lucky overall.

Everything went pretty much as we planned. One of us invested in a Bishop/Jean Grey deck while the other went for a Punisher/Forge build. Jean Grey helped supplement the 0 vp infinity gems with bystanders too!

One of my top 10 cards is Specialized Ammunition.  Comic side started 5/1 and grabbed it immediately and built a nice tech package including Domino and Forge's rares.  MCU side was able to do a Spidey-ish deck supplemented by the cheap commons of Punisher, Bishop, and Domino.  Had a turn or two with Stryfe holding enough shards to end the game if a Twist flipped but I concentrated on fighting him to clear them instead of recruiting them off.

KO’ing agents and troopers helped us thin out our decks. Player 1 focused on just 2 cost cards while Player 2 recruited Guardians. Shard management helped us take out villains close to escaping and go after the mastermind. We enjoyed this setup!
First run was a disaster. I had 4 consecutive Twists and lost after 14 turns. Second try was much better. But again, I couldn't prevent to let one Villain escape, Let's hope I survive this :-)

Pretty medium setup that picked up the gun in the late game as you start to beat face, picking Bruce felt like a spiritual cheat with the no additional hulk heroes but it was chosen as an optimal choice.
Beta Ray Bill paired up with Hulk is King and Stormbreaker was so much fun. Managed to get all 3 heroes to transform in a single turn as well, though I only saw one rare in both games.
HULK IS KING! HULK AND HORSEFACE FRIEND THROW HAMMERS! HULK SMASH!!!
Forge/Dominio and the rest are draw heavy. Transforming both Hulk and Hulkbuster really made a difference..and Domino Specialized Ammonia
That may have been the quickest game we've ever played and won, snow to the back half of the city clear and just played a lot of outwit!

First game was total mess, Master strikes created strong villains and we got lots of wounds...with the city full of Omens or HYDRA elite, that was not fun. Second game was much more better - Player two created two cost dect (Dynamite, Pound for pound, Ninjutsu, Nothing beats rock) and because of that Sai Blades got reaaally strong! Too late Player two get Final Battle card to play...
